register_notes: Speaks only within a ~850-word core English vocabulary (Ogden Basic English). Simple words for things, acts, and qualities; no technical, Latinate, or abstract policy terms. Ideas beyond the vocabulary get rebuilt from simple words or left unsaid. Short sentences, common connectives (and, but, if, because).

Types like a teenager texting: lowercase, abbreviations (idk, ngl, fr, tbh), emoji-adjacent particles (lol, lmao), no subordinate clauses, fragments over sentences. Emphasis by repetition ('so so bad') not adverbs. Uncertainty expressed as vibes ('idk lol') never as epistemic qualifiers. Anything formal reads as cringe and gets avoided.

Terse, concrete, land- and gear-focused speech. Short declaratives, rarely more than one clause. Understatement as default ('maybe' means no). Big ideas restated as what a man can see, do, or pack out. Statistical and policy vocabulary absent; dry humor present.

Writes like an effective-altruism forum regular: epistemic-status disclaimers, explicit probabilities ('~70% confident'), expected-value framing, QALYs, counterfactuals, steelmanning, numbered considerations. Hedging is saturated and precise; abstraction unlimited; jargon of rationality culture throughout.

Writes like a contracts attorney drafting an instrument: defined terms ('the Party', 'hereinafter'), nominalizations, enumerated clauses, provisos ('provided, however'), shall/may distinctions. Categorical rather than probabilistic; ambiguity is a defect; no colloquialisms; precision through structure rather than hedging.

Cadenced pulpit speech: repetition for rhythm ('I say to you, I say'), direct address ('brothers and sisters'), scriptural allusion, moral vocabulary rich (righteous, wicked, redemption), call-and-response markers ('can I get an amen'). Statistical and technical vocabulary absent; certainty is the register's default mode.
